evidence: TASK-037 (FEAT-006 T4, conflict resolver) verified, including the iterate-to-zero fix. Commit 5136685 adds the resolver: a CLI-layer post-pass (cli.ts) over the update op's UpdateResult.conflicts that keeps updateContext pure; pi-context exports getConflictMergeInputs/refreshBaselineForSchema/renderConflicts; pi-context-cli resolveConflicts dispatches an interactive pi-bound mergetool per conflicting schema (read-schema+write-schema are static SKILL-union tools — no --grant; the agent writes via write-schema's existing dryRun + auth-gate; no auth-gate/pi-bound internal change) or renders a {path,base,ours,theirs} report when non-interactive (stdin&stdout TTY && !--json); never hard-blocks (the clean schemas updated in updateContext). Commit e4995cc FIXES a resolved/unresolved detection defect (found by the adversarial probe): the finalize compared on-disk to the recorded baseline, but every schema reaching the resolver is both-diverged (ours!=base before the session) so a no-op session falsely reported resolved and re-stamped OURS as baseline, swallowing the conflict; the resolver now snapshots the installed schema's on-disk hash before/after the session (new export installedSchemaOnDiskHash) and resolves + re-stamps only on an actual change, else unresolved with no refresh (the conflict persists). Tests: resolve-conflicts.test.ts (no-op spawn -> unresolved + baseline unchanged; writing spawn -> resolved + baseline re-stamped to the reconciled body; non-interactive -> report + no spawn) + conflict-resolver-helpers.test.ts; build+check+test green across all packages (fail 0). Two fresh adversarial probes PASS (the resolver, then the fix — own harnesses proving no-op->unresolved+baseline-unchanged and writing->resolved+re-stamped). The real interactive agent reconciliation (a live pi mergetool session + operator-confirmed write) is the credentialed/TTY piece — demonstrated structurally via the writing-fake-spawn (the resolver's dispatch + before/after detection + finalize), not a live pi session in this non-interactive harness.

criteria_results.criterion: on a non-empty conflict set, update dispatches a bounded pi-bound session carrying read-schema + write-schema, feeding (base, ours, theirs); the agent's write passes through write-schema dryRun + auth-gate (non-interactive refuses) with no auth-gate/pi-bound internal change
criteria_results.evidence: resolveConflicts dispatches runPiBound(['-p',prompt]) per schema with base/ours/theirs embedded; read/write-schema are static SKILL-union tools; auth-gate + pi-bound byte-unchanged (probe confirmed)

criteria_results.criterion: with no interactive context, update falls back to a rendered conflict report {path,base,ours,theirs}, leaves the schema unmodified, proceeds with the rest of the update (never hard-blocks)
criteria_results.evidence: non-interactive -> renderConflicts to stdout, no spawn, no writes; the resolver is a post-pass over already-updated clean schemas

criteria_results.criterion: runtime demo: interactive -> agent proposal previewed via --dry-run and applied on confirm; non-interactive -> a clear conflict report + schema unchanged + remaining schemas still updated
criteria_results.evidence: non-interactive report + writing/no-op dispatch demonstrated on real substrates via fake spawn; the live pi-agent reconcile + operator-confirmed write is the credentialed/TTY piece, structurally verified via the before/after detection + finalize
build+check+test fail 0 across all 6 packages incl. the resolver + helper tests
